hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce charge features that disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) binding while being tolerated by SSTR2. These changes will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis is revised to emphasize the importance of disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to achieve SSTR2 selectivity. The focus will shift from optimizing ddG alone to optimizing Δmargin through targeted mutations.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.

Introducing polar or charged residues at positions 1 and 2 can disrupt conserved hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Hydrophobic substitutions at 5 and 6 can improve SSTR2-specific interactions. Polar substitutions at 11 and 12 can reduce off-target hydrogen bonding.
